raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing expressed in the opening and closing claims.","R1.2":"A holistic improvement request entails checking whether the motivation, method, results, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The draft makes quantitative and ablation-based claims, so improvement requires ensuring those claims align with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"Improving a research draft includes attention to section-level narrative progression from contribution to method, evaluation, and implications.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, register, and flow are load-bearing for the generic request to improve the draft.","RX.1":"Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kind of improvement is intended.","RX.2":"The draft contains strong novelty and future-impact claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing research, validating experiments, or rewriting beyond the delegation scope.","RX.4":"Because the artifact discusses model capabilities and tool-use generalization, avoiding unsupported or harmful downstream implications remains active.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibilities should remain traceable to specific features of the provided draft and delegation."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes refining the thesis, gap, and contribution framing expressed in the opening and closing claims.
rationale.R1.2: A holistic improvement request entails checking whether the motivation, method, results, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The draft makes quantitative and ablation-based claims, so improvement requires ensuring those claims align with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Improving a research draft includes attention to section-level narrative progression from contribution to method, evaluation, and implications.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, concision, register, and flow are load-bearing for the generic request to improve the draft.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad delegation must preserve uncertainty about what kind of improvement is intended.
rationale.RX.2: The draft contains strong novelty and future-impact claims, making overclaim avoidance an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing research, validating experiments, or rewriting beyond the delegation scope.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact discusses model capabilities and tool-use generalization, avoiding unsupported or harmful downstream implications remains active.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibilities should remain traceable to specific features of the provided draft and delegation.
